title: Credential Harvesting via EvilGinx Proxy - Executive Spearphish
description: Targeted spearphishing campaign against C-suite executives using EvilGinx2 reverse proxy to bypass MFA. Attackers cloned Microsoft 365 login page and intercepted real-time credentials and session tokens via adversary-in-the-middle proxy. T1557.002 T1566.002 T1539 T1056.006

evidence.content: Spearphishing email to CEO@company.com from 'IT Security & Support' <support@secure-portal-mfa.com>. Subject: 'Urgent: MFA re-enrollment required - action required within 24 hours'. Link: https://secure-portal-mfa.com/office365/login. DKIM: FAIL. SPF: softfail. T1566.002

DNS query for secure-portal-mfa.com from 10.10.0.25 (CEO workstation). Resolved to 185.220.101.42. Domain registered 3 days ago via Njalla. Registrar: Namecheap. WHOIS privacy enabled. T1566 T1583.001

TLS connection from 10.10.0.25 to 185.220.101.42:443. Certificate issued to *.secure-portal-mfa.com by Let's Encrypt. Actually serving a cloned Microsoft login page with real-time credential forwarding. T1557.002 T1583.002

Successful sign-in for CEO@company.com from IP 185.220.101.42 at 14:32 UTC. Authentication: MFA claim completed. Device: Windows (Unmanaged). Country: Estonia (unusual). No Conditional Access policy matched due to missing device compliance. T1539 T1078 T1557.002

Mailbox rule created by CEO@company.com: 'Forward all incoming email to ceo.forward2024@gmail.com'. Rule type: Inbox -> redirect. Created 15 seconds after sign-in from 185.220.101.42. T1564.002 T1114.003

Session token replay detected from IP 45.33.22.115 at 14:33 UTC. Token was issued 60 seconds earlier to IP 185.220.101.42. 42 API calls made: Mail.Read, Mail.Send, Contacts.Read, User.Read.All. T1539 T1528

Execution of update_checker.exe on CEO workstation at 14:31 UTC. Downloaded from https://secure-portal-mfa.com/update_checker.exe. SHA256: f1e2d3c4b5a6978876543210abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef01. Binary modifies hosts file to add entry: 127.0.0.1 security.microsoft.com. T1562.001 T1071.001

Registry modification: HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\Security\AllowClickOnceTrustLevel set to 0x1. Enables execution of ClickOnce applications from the cloned phishing site. T1112 T1204.002

5 sensitive documents downloaded via API from CEO OneDrive: '2025_Strategic_Plan.pptx', 'M&A_Target_Analysis.xlsx', 'Board_Compensation.ods', 'Q4_Earnings_Draft.pdf', 'Cybersecurity_Audit_Findings.docx'. Exfiltrated at 14:35 UTC to IP 45.33.22.115. T1539 T1567.002
